figures.caption: Fig. 5: Action Graph Workflow on sausage cutting, consisting of an On-Trigger Node, a Computation Node, and a State Update Node, connected in an ordered way.
Fig. 6: Teleoperation Methods. (Left) We use Joystick and Keyboard to teleoperate XLeRobot, and (Right) LeaderFollower Teleoperation for LeRobot.
TABLE I: Features Comparisons across different open-source robotics environment.
Fig. 8: Real-world execution on diverse household tasks.

Fig. 2: The Architecture of LeHome. (Left) LeHome Assets can deliver realistic simulations of different robots, articulation/rigid objects, deformable objects, and cover multiple household scenarios. (Right) Leveraging diverse simulation methods and mechanisms, LeHome Engine enables various simulation capabilities. (Bottom) Subsequently, LeHome Benchmark utilizes these assets to construct tasks, conduct domain randomization, and facilitate teleoperation data collection.
Fig. 3: Simulated Deformble Objects cover 6 categories with visually and physically high-fidelity assets for each category.

Fig. 4: Diverse Manipulation Mechanisms. LeHome models causal relationships of manipulation through the action graph, ensuring the simulation results align with real-world causal relationships and providing high-fidelity interactions.
